response:
# Decision: v1 geocoding sunset

**Option chosen: A (firm sunset), with the date and outreach calibrated to what the complaints actually show — not the memo's "e.g. six months" placeholder.**

## Terms
- **Sunset date:** 2027-04-14 (9 months from today, 2026-07-14) — not 6 months. NorthRoute's ask isn't a config change, it's a parser rewrite plus "a full QA pass on maps our dispatchers use live" (complaints.md). A live-dispatcher-facing rewrite-and-QA cycle is a real project; 6 months was never derived from that timeline, it was a round number in the memo. 9 months is still a guess, but it's a guess anchored to the one concrete scope description we have, not to convenience.
- **Usage threshold:** dropped as a *timing* input. 0.4% justifies committing to termination at all (this is genuinely a thin, unowned tail, so killing it is safe to commit to) — but it does not set the date, because the complaints directly show traffic share doesn't track migration cost. C is rejected on this basis.
- **Notice schedule:** immediate, multi-channel notice today to every account with v1 traffic in the last full month, not just the three who wrote in — email + in-dashboard banner + direct outreach to top-volume v1 accounts. Reminders at T-6mo, T-3mo, T-30d, T-7d. This exists specifically because the three complainants are the engaged fraction of a "long, thin tail: no single large customer, mostly old integrations nobody ever updated" (MEMO.md) — the accounts most likely to miss a deadline are the ones not currently emailing anyone.
- **Hands-on help:** dedicated engineering support offered to NorthRoute, Tavo, and Ferrymark. Extensions beyond 2027-04-14 are available case-by-case only to accounts that have demonstrably started migrating and can show a specific blocker — not as a blanket delay to any account that hasn't started, since "no bandwidth this year" (Tavo) and "nobody has time right now" (Ferrymark) are exactly the kind of open-ended objections that never resolve on their own and would collapse this into option B.
- **B is rejected outright:** the memo's own framing is that every bugfix is already written and tested twice, and "until complaints stop" has no exit condition — vivid complaints about load-bearing systems don't stop on their own, so B guarantees the double-maintenance cost keeps compounding indefinitely.

## Load-bearing assumption
**Per-customer migration cost, for the realistic population of v1 integrations, fits inside a 9-month runway when paired with hands-on help for accounts that engage.** If false — if work like NorthRoute's shape rewrite plus live-dispatcher QA systematically runs longer than 9 months even with direct engineering support — this decision reverses, because the firm date stops being a deadline and becomes a scheduled breakage event.

## Failure class accepted
Some fraction of the unengaged tail — old integrations with no current owner, notices landing in inboxes nobody watches — will not migrate in time and will break at cutover, discovered after the fact rather than pre-empted. This is accepted as *bounded* breakage: 0.4% of traffic, no single large customer, multi-channel outreach specifically built to shrink that fraction. It's accepted in preference to the alternative failure class guaranteed by B: unresolved, compounding double-maintenance cost with no forcing function, and the team "circling back" on this exact question again in another six months.
